title: Systemic Trust

content.question: Where are you tempted to extract short-term value at long-term trust's cost?
sop: Write down every explicit and implicit promise your business makes to customers.
Audit the last 90 days for any moment you cut a corner on one of those promises.
Rate your Consistency and Integrity honestly, 1-10 each (see calculation, page 4), based on how often you kept the promise even when it cost you.
Document your standards in writing so trust doesn't depend on memory or mood.
Refuse the short-term extraction option once this month, even when it's the easier or more profitable choice.
questions: Where are you tempted to extract short-term value at long-term trust's cost?
What's one promise your business makes that you've quietly stopped fully keeping?
If every customer from the last 90 days compared notes, would your story hold up the same way to all of them?
content.equation_example: Rate 1-10: Consistency, Integrity. Time equals years in relationship with the customer/market (count). Consistency 8 times Time 3 times Integrity 9 equals 216. Higher and rising means trust compounds into referrals and repeat business; lower means every transaction has to be re-earned from zero.
content.closing_statement: Every shortcut taken today is remembered tomorrow.

caption:
Principle Nº 175: Systemic Trust

Long-term trust beats short-term extraction.

Ask yourself: Where are you tempted to extract short-term value at long-term trust's cost?
